name.text: Finding the distance and displacement of a hiker navigating through a flat forest area using a compass

questiontext.text:
A hiker is navigating through a flat forest area using a compass. The hiker performs the following movements from their base camp:

  • First, they walk \({@d_east@}\) km East to reach a lookout point.
  • From the lookout point, they then walk \({@d_north@}\) km North to reach a waterfall.
  1. What is the total distance the hiker travelled from the base camp to the waterfall? [[input:ans1]] km [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]
  2. What is the magnitude of the hiker's displacement (the straight-line distance) from the base camp to the waterfall? [[input:ans2]] km [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]
[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-2, 15, 15, -2], axis: true, showCopyright: false, defaultAxes: { x: { name: 'East (km)', withLabel: true }, y: { name: 'North (km)', withLabel: true, label: { rotate: 90, offset: [-30, 10] } } } }); var e_val = {#d_east#}; var n_val = {#d_north#}; // Start and Key Points var p1 = board.create('point', [0, 0], {name: 'Base Camp', fixed: true, size: 3, color: 'black'}); var p2 = board.create('point', [e_val, 0], {name: 'Lookout', size: 2, color: 'blue'}); var p3 = board.create('point', [e_val, n_val], {name: 'Waterfall', size: 3, color: 'red'}); // Path taken board.create('arrow', [p1, p2], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('arrow', [p2, p3], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 2}); // Displacement Vector board.create('segment', [p1, p3], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'green', name: 'Displacement', withLabel: true}); [[/jsxgraph]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

To analyze the hiker's motion, we distinguish between the path taken (distance) and the change in position (displacement):

1. Distance Travelled

Distance is the total length of the hiker's path:

\(\begin{align} \text{Distance} &= \text{Distance East} + \text{Distance North} \\ &= {@d_east@} \text{ km} + {@d_north@} \text{ km} \\ &= {@ta1@} \text{ km} \end{align}\) 2. Displacement

Because the hiker turned at a 90° angle (from East to North), the displacement is the hypotenuse of a right-angled triangle. We use the Pythagorean theorem:

\(\begin{align} \text{Displacement} &= \sqrt{(\text{East})^2 + (\text{North})^2} \\ &= \sqrt{({@d_east@})^2 + ({@d_north@})^2} \\ &= \sqrt{{@disp_sq@}} \\ &= {@ta2@} \text{ km} \end{align}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
